tspan.tspan: 50cm x 50cm
Setup
• Each player gets 11 markers of a color.
• There are also 3 shared white markers and 4 six-sided dice.
Playing the Game
• When it’s your turn, roll the 4 dice and take the 3 white markers.
• Divide the dice into two pairs of your choosing.
• Take the added up number of a pair and do one of the following:
a) Place a white marker
at the beginning of the column of the added up
number OR
b) If you already have a marker of your color in that column, place a
white
marker
on the square above that cone OR
c) If you already have a white marker in a column, move that marker up the
column by
one.
• After each roll, if you ever can’t do at least one of these actions because you
ran out of white marker to place and you rolled the number of a column where
you don’t have one
of the three white marker, you remove all three white
markers from the board and your
turn is over. All progress is lost.
• Before you roll the dice or after you’ve used at least one pair of dice, you can
choose to
stop
. Replace the white markers on the board with markers of your
color.
• Winning a Column:
• Once a player has reached the top of board with a white marker and has
decided to stop, they have won the column.
• Remove all other markers from that column.
• That column can no longer be played on.
Winning The Game
• Be the first player to win three columns.
Can't Stop
